CONTENT.Honours: PSVKNVB Cup: 2021–22Johan Cruyff Shield: 2021, 2022GermanySummer Olympics Silver medal: 2016IndividualEredivisie Team of the Month: March 2022,
CONTENT.Club career: Max joined Schalke 04 in 2010 from Bayern Munich. He made his Bundesliga debut on 25 March 2014 against Borussia Dortmund, coming in for Julian Draxler.On 30 April 2014, he signed a three-year contract with Karlsruher SC, effective the following season.On 4 August 2015, Max joined FC Augsburg on a two-year contract with an option to extend the agreement, for a reported fee of €3.6 million. Max scored his first goal for Augsburg in a 4–0 victory overHamburger SV in the Bundesliga on 30 April 2017. He finished the 2017–18 Bundesliga season with 2 goals and 12 assists for Augsburg With 12 assists, he became the 2nd top assist provider in the league only behind Bayern's Thomas Müller who had 14 assists. In December 2018, in a 2–2 draw with Hertha BSC, he made his 100th league appearance for Augsburg. On 13 December 2019, Max scored a brace away to TSG Hoffenheim while playing on the left wing, as regular winger Ruben Vargas was serving a one-game suspension. Max scored another brace in their next game, a win over Fortuna Düsseldorf, also while on the wing.On 2 September 2020, Max joined PSV Eindhoven.On 31 January 2023, Max moved to Eintracht Frankfurt on loan with an option to buy. On 26 May 2023, Eintracht activated their option to buy and made the transfer permanent, signing a three-year contract with Max.On 6 August 2024, Max signed a three-year contract with Panathinaikos in Greece.
CONTENT.Personal life: He is the son of former German international striker Martin Max.

CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 5 August 2024As of match played 17 November 2020
CONTENT.International career: He was part of the squad for the 2016 Summer Olympics, where Germany won the silver medal. He earned his first call-up for the senior team on 6 November 2020. His debut came on 11 November 2020, in a friendly game against the Czech Republic.

SUMMARY: Philipp Martin Max (German pronunciation: [ˈfiːlɪp ˈmaks]; born 30 September 1993) is a German footballer who plays as a left-back for Greek Super League club Panathinaikos.
